0: Hired onto the season's first support caravan, you trek the newly thawed Imperial Pass toward the remote, snowbound outpost of Frosthaven, where a hungry pack of predators is now circling your group, and you must fight them off to get everyone through safely.
1: Arriving as the season's first relief team at the remote, snowbound outpost of Frosthaven, the mercenaries crest the final ridge to find the settlement ablaze and besieged by hulking Algox raiders, and wade in to drive them off.
2: Following the assault on the town, the party heads into the Copperneck Mountains, slipping toward the Algox stronghold through a side passage to learn why the creatures struck and to deal with the threat they pose.
3: Rather than quietly scouting the nearby Algox, the party takes the aggressive route and marches straight to the threshold of the frost-apes' stronghold to break their growing threat head-on.
4A: Having ventured into the Copperneck Mountains following the Algox conflict, the party arrives to face the warring forces there and must fight their way through the icy stronghold to settle matters with the rival tribe.
4B: Having chosen to scout the Algox stronghold rather than storm it head-on, the party slips in through a rear passage deep in the Copperneck Mountains, hoping to learn what's driving the tribe's fury and find a way to defuse the conflict.
5: Chasing a fleeing Algox north of the Whitefire Wood, the party crashes through cracking ice into a buried crypt, where they must fight off icy guardians and shatter the altar fueling its undead defenders.
6: Acting on Satha's report of a "crab problem," you trek west along the frigid, ice-choked shore of the Biting Sea, scanning the coast for traces of the enormous crustaceans behind the trouble.
7: With a fragile truce holding between Frosthaven and the Algox, the party ventures into the flooded sea caves to find out why the lurkers there have suddenly turned hostile.
8: Pushing into an icy valley in search of the strange machines, the party finds itself ambushed in a narrow crystal-walled trench, with wolves closing from behind and bears and imps barring the way forward. The goal is to fight through and reach the far end.
9: Venturing underground into a network of glowing, rune-marked catacombs, the party must fight through restless undead and ever-multiplying oozes while pressing deeper toward a mysterious sealed chamber.
10: Having chased the fleeing quarry into a vast expanse of runic ice pillars, the party presses deeper through the frozen, shield-charged terrain, aiming to corner and defeat every enemy lurking among the crystal spires.
11: After climbing through the Algox tunnels of Snowscorn Mountain, the party follows a narrow cliffside trail to the summit at the Icespeaker chief's request, aiming to confront and wipe out the hostile Snowspeakers gathered there.
12: Following scout reports of strange mechanical wanderers, the party journeys east into the frozen Whitefire Wood to investigate an ancient ruin, only to find its temple steps guarded by long-dormant automatons stirring back to life.
13: Having chosen to push along the coast rather than build a boat after the journey's earlier leg, the party must cross a treacherous icy inlet, starting split across separate paths and fighting forward before a fragile ice bridge buckles under the assault.
14: Venturing out to a treacherous stretch of coastline crawling with eels and piranha pigs, the party must fight across the flooded ground and tear down a towering rock pillar before the endless swarm overwhelms them.
15: Pressing deeper into the ruins, the party climbs to confront a Chaos Lieutenant atop an ancient tower, racing a one-use elevator and rooms full of foes to bring the commander down before it can repair itself.
16: Riding a rickety elevator down into an abandoned mechanical facility, the party must hold together through a long, hazardous descent while ruined machines and hidden artillery awaken to stop them from reaching the depths below.
17: Hunting the last missing shard of the Lurkers Crown, the party descends into a sealed vault of six locked chambers, working pressure plates to open each room and pressing deeper to recover what they seek.
18: Pressing deeper into the Algox storyline, the party leaves its sled behind to cross a glittering field of ice-crystal conduits littered with frozen Algox dead, only to find unsettling creatures stirring the corpses back to life.
19: High in the Copperneck Mountains, the party reaches a haunted hall where restless spirits stir. They must quiet the spirits' rising fury before that rage shakes the peaks apart.
20: Pressing deeper, the party enters a temple where strange mechanical worshippers cluster around a glowing orb sealed behind a shimmering barrier; their goal is to bring down that shroud and shut the orb down before the awakened machines overwhelm them.
21: Lured into a host's frostbound mansion and then turned upon, the party must battle through a series of ice-choked rooms full of frost creatures and traps to confront the powerful entity behind the ambush.
22: While crossing the Biting Sea, the party's ship comes under assault by Lurkers drawn to the crown shards they carry, forcing the crew to repel the boarders and keep the vessel afloat.
23: After breaching the ancient spire, the party descends into its basement expecting machines but instead finds it crawling with undead, and must clear out every last enemy lurking in the chambers below.
24: Having leapt into a teleporting beam of light at the close of the last fight, the party finds itself at the foot of a tower crackling with chaotic energy, and must battle upward floor by floor.
25: Drawn underground by an alarm blaring from a network of decaying mechanical passages, the party descends into the rust-choked tunnels to silence the disturbance and deal with the malfunctioning machines roused by their intrusion.
26: Pressing deeper into the abandoned machine works straight from the elevator, the party fights through a single chamber of rogue constructs while keeping every figure clear of a deadly central pressure plate the machines are trying to trigger.
27: Pressing deeper into the Lurkers' subterranean lair, the party descends through a sprawling, mind-bending complex of chambers, where their goal is to seek out and shatter the altars hidden across its depths.
28: A secret peace summit between the two feuding Algox factions has erupted into chaos, with hostile warriors from both sides flooding the cavern. Our job is to shield the two faction leaders and get them safely out alive.
29: Having thrown in with one Algox war faction over brokering peace, the party splits across the battlefield to bring down the rival clan's magical spire before its defenders can rally.
30: Having thrown in with the warlike Algox faction rather than brokering peace, the party splits up to storm the enemy clan's stronghold and tear down the magical spire before it can be brought to bear.
31: Pressing deeper into the mountain temple, the party advances through an ornate stone passage laced with silver tubing, where spinning flame-wreathed constructs stand in their way. Their goal is to fight through and reach the far exit.
32: Tracking a Fiend Shriek infestation into the Radiant Forest, the party pushes through the creatures' lair to reach and destroy the roost that keeps spawning them.
33: In a stretch of forest where the deep freeze has receded, the party pushes through the lurking creatures of the wood to track down hidden radiant stones by triangulating their positions.
34: Having climbed the spire, the party now faces the Lord of Chaos in its chamber, fighting past a barrier of chaotic energy and waves of summoned creatures to bring the boss down.
35: Escorting a small companion into a junkyard, the party must keep him safe from the hostile machines and undead lurking among the wreckage while he digs through the heaps of discarded scrap for anything useful.
36: Pressing deeper into the buried duct tunnels as part of unraveling the Spire mystery, the party must fight through relentless waves of foes to seal off four doorways before they are overwhelmed.
37: Roused in the dead of night by the glowing apparition of a strange Aesther woman, the party follows her summons out beyond the walls into a desolate stretch of wilderness to uncover what she wants of them.
38: Continuing the Algox storyline, the party splits up to guide two emissaries safely across hostile, beast-infested ground, fending off hounds and a polar bear to deliver them to a sacred altar.
39: Arriving at the Icespeakers' Algox encampment, the party finds the survivors overrun by Frozen Corpses and Black Imps, and must fight through the besieged camp to clear out every attacker.
40: After a sealed, tar-coated crate built to hold elemental energy turned up aboard an abandoned ship at the Frosthaven docks, the party sets out to investigate where it came from and deal with whatever its discovery has stirred up.
41: Carrying the strange, whispering coral shard recovered earlier, the party follows its pull away from Frosthaven, escorting it northward along the frozen coast toward the open sea as it urges them onward.
42: Pressing onward along the Lurkers' undersea path, the party descends into a vast lightless trench teeming with deep-dwelling horrors, aiming to plunder five caches of treasure hidden among its rooms rather than fight everything off.
43: Armed with a powerful one-use EMP weapon, the party must fight their way across a machine-choked battlefield, racing to the far edge of the map before relentless robotic foes overwhelm them.
44: Pushing deep into the Unfettered machines' tower, the party advances through its inner chambers to root out the hostile constructs at their source, fighting through the rooms to wipe out the enemy forces and smash the six metal cabinets driving them.
45: Continuing the Algox storyline, the party travels to a frozen, glacier-bound coast to harvest ancient ice, one of the components needed to lift the curse troubling the Algox people.
46: Trekking through a frozen mountain pass to aid the Algox, the party must harvest ancient snow needed to break a curse troubling the community, all while fending off the dangerous creatures that haunt the route.
47: Guarding a struggling caravan as it pushes across a frozen, wind-scoured highland, the party must beat back the pack of predators harrying the travelers and clear a path over the ridge before nightfall.
48: Tracing crumbled coastal walls up a long-abandoned, storm-battered island, the party climbs stone steps into the ruins, only to be set upon by a swarm of wind demons they must fight off to press on.
49: After steering the bathysphere into an undersea cavern to escape pursuing Lurkers and sealing the entrance behind you, the party is now trapped deep below the surface and must press onward through the flooded depths with air running short.
50: A pulse-emitting device is sinking through a flooded cavern, and the party must descend alongside it, keeping pace and fending off lurking creatures to protect the machine all the way down.
51: Having battled deep into the Unfettered automatons' complex, the party reaches the platform overlooking its core to confront the machine leader called the Orphan, aiming to disable the central control console.
52: Still chasing the source of the Algoxes' senseless brutality, the party reaches an ancient altar deep in the ice and must cleanse it, clearing out the imps and daemons guarding the site.
53: Following the trail of the crown shards into a sunken palace, the party confronts the monstrous ruler of the deep and its swarming guardians, aiming to subdue the throne's keeper and seize the shard before being overwhelmed.
54: Searching the flooded ruins of the Lurker garrison, the party must dig through the submerged debris to recover a buried coral shard while fending off the hostile creatures stirred up amid the wreckage.
55: Reaching the climax of the Algox storyline, the party sets out to break the curse afflicting the Algox by destroying a massive crystal, all while taking care not to harm the bewitched creatures themselves.
56: Deep in an abandoned, cold cavern, the party carries Crain's device meant to reinforce the failing seals on a sealed shadow-beast. With its menace looming over them, they must press through guardian foes to confront the Harbinger directly.
57: Trapped by a savage storm on a frozen mountain peak, the party faces the Render, a wind-shrouded beast guarding the heights, and must use a companion's wind carvings to strip away its protective barrier and bring it down.
58: After everything the machines have put us through, we face the Orphan one last time inside its inner sanctum, picking our way past hulking pillars to reach the central control console and shut it down.
59: After brokering peace with the Orphan, the party must escort the intelligent machine deeper into the spire to reprogram the control cabinets, protecting it from the rebellious automaton guards who refuse to lay down arms.
60: Having gathered the six scattered shards over the campaign, the party now faces the towering guardian and its ethereal lieutenants standing between them and the reforged crown, and must bring it down to claim their prize.
61: Plunged into an eerie, otherworldly realm, the party fights beside a fierce Orchid warrior named Moonshard as demons and imps swarm in. The goal is to clear out every foe while keeping Moonshard alive.
62: In the crystal fields, the party guards Crain while he operates a strange crystal-powered device to draw out the seal's energy, holding off waves of demons and other foes long enough for him to finish.
63: High in the Copperneck Mountains, the party accompanies the engineer Crain as he works on a device meant to counter the Savvas threat, and must hold off waves of attackers long enough to keep him alive and finish the job.
64: Demonic forces have broken in upon Frosthaven itself, so the party must hold the line beside the city's guard captains and militia, fending off waves of attackers while keeping the townsfolk and a key ally alive.
65: Having answered a plea from a cluster of displaced Aesther beings stranded between planes, the party fights through chamber after chamber of undead and imps to reach and defend a mysterious orb at journey's end.
66: Pressing deeper into a place charged with raw elemental power, the party must reach and activate a set of elemental cores, fighting through the forces guarding them to harness that energy.
67: Continuing the Blinkblade's personal quest, the party gathers at four arcane pillars to attune a powerful Core, and must hold this central ground while demons pour in from both sides for twelve rounds.
68: After working through the stages of the Aether Outpost quest line, the party confronts a powerful demonic entity known as the Face of Torment and its summoned minions, aiming to bring the venture to its decisive end.
69: To fulfill a companion's personal quest, the party ventures into the Radiant Forest, fighting through imps, drakes, and hulking Earth Daemons to harvest three soil samples that fall when the daemons are slain.
70: Word has reached us that zealots intend to burn down a revered ancient tree deep in the Radiant Forest. We've come to stand guard, fending off the attackers while keeping the True Oak itself from harm.
71: A brawl between rival aquatic raiders has broken out at Frosthaven's docks, leaving the dockmaster trapped amid the chaos. The party must wade into the fighting and get them safely out before time runs short.
72: An artifact lies frozen inside a massive block of ice that can't be freed where it sits, so the party must shove the unwieldy slab toward the exit while fending off the automatons guarding it.
73: With their boat sunk, the party is stranded in open water and must paddle across drifting wreckage, fending off eels and other aquatic foes as they push toward shore.
74A: Following clues toward a long-sought artifact, the party must pick its way down a narrow, flood-soaked path, felling trees to bridge the water while hostile archers harry them from the far bank.
74B: Chasing a long-sought relic, the party reaches a narrow trail swallowed by floodwater and must fell trees to bridge the gaps while Algox archers pelt them from across the way.
75: Pressing deep into the Fish King's lair, the party picks its way along a narrow, energy-charged path to plant explosive charges that seal off enemy reinforcements and bring down the creatures guarding the way.
76: The Fish King is ascending to godhood, drawing on chaotic energy that leaves him partly incorporeal. The party must confront him in a multi-phase showdown to cut down his power and stop his transformation before it completes.
77: A monstrous Fish King has risen to claim its power, and the party must confront the towering creature and its glowing orb in a desperate boss battle to bring it down before its ascension is complete.
78: A jittery, hooded stranger has begged you to settle a score with the Lurkers, so you head into a flooded cavern to cut through the creatures lurking there and recover the artifact they guard.
79: Having taken on a job posted at the Town Hall to further your own ends, you press into a frozen expanse and must guide a heavy sled to the rear of the cavern to batter through an ice wall, fending off frost creatures along the way.
80: Joining the Algox on what is meant to be a peaceful mission rather than a fight, the party must break into a guarded inner sanctum and reach the ancient Relic housed within, all while avoiding harm to the Algox brethren standing in the way.
81: Following leads about the unnatural blizzard gripping the region, the party ventures into the crumbling remains of an old research lab to seek out Voice-of-Eight and the answers hidden among the wreckage.
82: A celebrated band of explorers set out for the frozen north and vanished in an unnatural blizzard, so we've trekked out to dig the freezing survivors from the snow, revive them, and haul everyone back out before the storm claims us too.
83: After a cartographer reports a strange, fish-reeking cavern in the Imperial Mountains, the party ventures inside to learn what lurks there and clear out the briny creatures that have taken up residence.
84: Having tracked a livestock-devouring slime back to its lair, the party ventures into the cave to corner the gelatinous predator and finally put an end to the menace at its source.
85: After days tracking a hidden idol across treacherous, avalanche-swept mountain trails, you finally close in on its resting place and must retrieve the surrounding treasures and slip back out alive.
86: Answering a job-board contract, the party ventures out to track down a spectral Ice Wraith known as the Lady in White, fighting through her summoned imps and demons to put the haunting spirit to rest.
88: Having already disrupted the Collector's prized hoard once before, the party now tracks down this sinister hobbyist for a final reckoning, aiming to put him down before he can add them to his collection.
89: Deep in the Copperneck Mountains, the party ventures into a facility where a dangerous fire is building around the crystals, and must press through the burning chambers to contain the blaze before it erupts beyond control.
90: Following up on Satha's giant-crab problem, the party treks west along the frozen, treacherous shore of the Biting Sea, hunting for signs of the enormous crustaceans amid the icy waters.
91: After a treacherous landing of the rowboat on a thin strip of beach between towering cliffs and the churning sea, the party finds Lurkers closing in from both sides and must fight their way along the shore to escape.
92: An abandoned, supply-laden vessel is foundering out in the bay, so the party races aboard to haul away as much cargo as they can before the whole wreck slips beneath the waves.
93A: A late-night dispute at the rebuilt Crater tavern boils over into open combat between two rival factions, leaving your mercenaries caught in the middle and forced to fight their way through the chaos.
93B: At the Crater, the burned-out ruin of an old tavern that's drawn restless crowds for several nights running, the party wades into a chaotic clash between two rival factions whose feuding leaders are fixated on tearing each other apart.
94: Guided by a frightened Vermling whose family was seized by machines deep in the Whitefire Woods, the party storms the well-guarded facility to free the captives and track down the missing painter.
95: Grim discoveries among the dead lead the party to take charge of a sarcophagus, escorting and maneuvering it across hostile ground to lay the fallen properly to rest.
96: Deep in the tunnels beneath the city, the party reaches a long-abandoned transit chamber and must board a slowly drifting floating platform, riding it toward the far exit while grabbing what loot they can and surviving whatever stirs in the dark.
97: Having tracked the threat to its source, the party breaks into the hub that commands the region's machines, where they can seize the controls and turn the automatons against their master.
98: Answering a job posting, the party ventures into an unstable underground vent that begins caving in around them, so they must fight through the creatures lurking inside while racing to escape before the collapsing tunnels crush anyone left behind.
99: Trapped inside a heavily guarded prison, the party must work together to trigger the door mechanisms and fight their way out past the ranged sentries holding them captive.
100: Having pushed through the wrought-iron gates, the party takes momentary refuge from the raging storm in the ruins of a once-grand entrance hall, where they must contend with the swirling swarms closing in around them.
101: A creeping darkness deep in the tunnels has been driving the Algox to madness, so the party presses down into the Harrower passages to hunt for the root of the corruption.
102: A fierce storm has seized your vessel and is driving it down the coast, far from Frosthaven's sheltered harbor, so the party must hold together against the battering winds and fight their way toward the ship's edge to escape.
103: Having earlier discovered a sealed lead trapdoor hidden at the bottom of an empty chest, the party now returns to pry it open and descend into the pillared chamber waiting beneath.
104: A key recovered from the Glowing Catacombs opens a way into a strange pocket realm where spring and winter sit side by side, divided by clashing elements; the party must press across this dual-season expanse to reach the rewards waiting at the far end.
105: An elemental puzzle leads the party into a newly uncovered cave where wind and fire hold sway, and they must press through its hazard-filled chambers and clear out the creatures lurking among the ruins.
106: After matching crude markings on a salvaged alloy and mold to features in the landscape, the party reaches the snow-buried mural they uncovered and works to open its hidden mechanism and explore the long-lost forge beyond.
107: Setting out on a repeatable side venture, the party delves through a string of chambers in successive episodes, pressing deeper room by room and clearing each one to claim resources of their choosing.
108: After deciphering an ancient painted stone that maps a real, treasure-laden location, the party tracks down the depicted site and ventures into the glittering pit to claim its riches.
109: A strange device the party recovered has projected a map pointing to a hidden factory, so they venture inside to investigate its machinery and clear out the mechanical defenders guarding the place.
110: Pressing deeper beneath the mountain, the party enters an ancient temple raised around a sealed-away source of power and its watchful guardian, fighting through the sanctuary to confront what protects it.
111: Chasing the promise of treasure, a damaged magic mirror has pulled the party into a strange dimensional pocket split into two mirrored halves. They must fight their way through this reflected prison and overcome the entity trapped within.
112: A pack of wolves has been driven from its den, so the party teams up with the displaced hounds to clear out the creatures that took over their home. Keep the wolf allies alive while routing the intruders.
113: Following the trail of the long-lost Pirate Queen, the party ventures into a damp cavern where a carved stone lock guards her secrets, aiming to use the gathered coins to open it and learn where her treasure lies.
114: Seeking a route through the Copperneck Mountains toward ancient ruins beyond, the party takes a job from a crew of tinkerers to clear the wildlife choking a mountain pass by smashing the ice pillars blocking the way.
115: Hired by the tinkerer Pinter Droman to help carve a new mountain trade route, you must now guard the support pillars his crew erected as an angry Algox tribe sweeps in to tear them down. Hold off the assault and keep at least one pillar standing.
116: The newly carved mountain trade route finally opens, but bandits ambush the merchant convoy on its inaugural run. The party must escort the caravan of carriages along the road and keep the highwaymen from destroying them.
117: With polar bears driven into Whitefire Wood and thinning out the local wildlife, the Trapper has asked the party to round up the displaced bears and guide them toward a habitat where they can settle and recover.
118: Following up on disturbing reports of necromantic magic among the Lurkers, the party wades into a swarm of amulet-bearing undead that keep rising again, racing to seize the amulets that fuel their reanimation.
119: Venturing deep into the Radiant Forest, the party must guard a vulnerable companion, the Infuser, escorting them safely through the hostile woods to complete their work.
120: A strange glowing orb has been radiating waves that warp the minds of nearby creatures and people, driving them to act erratically. The party ventures in to locate and destroy that source before its influence spreads further.
121: Beneath the waves, corrupted Lurkers stand guard over a coral-rimmed crater hiding a shard of an ancient crown, so the party deploys the tinkerer's psychic device to break the creatures' thrall and claim the relic.
122: Venturing into an icy lair, the party comes face to face with an insatiable, ever-hungry creature called the Hungry Maw and must decide whether to fight it head-on or satisfy its endless appetite.
123: Having scaled the legs of a massive walking war machine, the party now battles Algox warriors holding its shoulder, racing to defeat them and force open a hatch into the titan before it can be turned against a settlement.
124: A psychic warning points the party to a shard on the ocean floor, where a dark corruption has drawn swarms of Lurkers to guard and spread it. The party dives in to confront the gathering horde and stop the threat from growing.
125: A tense exchange with the Lurkers over the coveted amulets turns to conflict, and the party must fight through the foes here while wrestling with a cursed feline idol that slows whoever carries it.
126: An ice-cream vendor named Joseph set up shop in a haunted field outside Frosthaven and got stranded as spirits emerged, so the party crosses the dangerous ground to recover his scattered goods and hand them off to frightened townsfolk waiting at the edge.
127: A ghostly pirate vessel crewed by wraiths has appeared after the party meddled with a cursed artifact. Now they must board the haunted freighter, plunder its scattered cargo crates, and slip away before anyone drops.
128: Reunited with the brothers Nits and Narb at a Frosthaven tavern, you spin tales of past exploits for a new acquaintance, replaying a wild story round by round as you rise to each challenge it conjures.
129: Trekking back westward toward Frosthaven under a darkening, snowy sky, the party joins forces with the city guard to set a trap and spring an ambush on an incoming enemy force before it can reach the settlement.
130: After securing the gems and worrying about the approaching militia, the ground gives way and the party tumbles into a ravine, scattering their containers. Now you must recover the gems and battle the circling shrike fiends to escape.
131: Following visions of a distant peninsula, the party edges across a narrow ice bridge spanning the open ocean until it broadens into a frozen plateau, where they must confront the Lurkers waiting there.
132: Drawn to an ancient temple said to hold a powerful feline idol, the party ventures inside past its guardians and traps to recover the prized artifact and carry it back out.
133: Deep in a crumbling temple in the Imperial Mountains, a golden cat-shaped idol has been uncovered and the cavern is collapsing around you. Grab its blessing and make for the exit before the whole place comes down.
134: At the librarian Eddica's urging, the party speaks an incantation that conjures a crumbling stone tower from thin air, then ventures inside to uncover the ancient knowledge sealed within.
135: Acting on Eddica's guidance, the party ventures northwest and speaks an incantation that conjures a hidden fortress from the snow. Inside, they must navigate its two linked planes to confront the imprisoned spellcaster Belara.
136: Following a Vermling barkeep's tip, the party slips into a hidden cliffside cave and finds a flooded cavern with moored log rafts. Though seemingly abandoned, splashing and chittering reveal lurking monsters the party must wipe out.
137: Chasing the legendary fortune of a long-dead pirate queen, the party follows Dinah's dinghy through an illusory cliff into a hidden sea cave, where disturbed creatures stir in the icy passages and must be fought off.
